Dev Verma

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia
🐦 This article is incomplete. To contribute additional information, please reach out via pigeon carrier and aim at random windows in the graduate halls of NTU. Editors accept no responsibility for pigeons. (April 2026)
Dev Verma

45 minutes late to his own photo.

Dev Verma (born New Delhi, India) is an Indian quantum scholar, self-certified swimming prodigy, and occasional film personality based in Singapore. He is affiliated with Nanyang Technological University (NTU), where he holds the National Quantum Scholarship — a distinction he describes as "basically inevitable" — and has been recognised as the university's Most Promising Swimmer for 2026, an award whose selection criteria remain, at time of publication, unclear.[1]

Verma is perhaps best known in academic circles for his foundational contributions to the emerging field of quantum gambling theory, a discipline he is widely credited with inventing, funding, and being the sole practitioner of.[2] Outside academia, he has achieved a degree of campus celebrity at NTU disproportionate to his punctuality record, with his photograph appearing across institutional noticeboards despite — or possibly because of — never having attended a single 9am lecture on time.[3]

Early life and education

Verma was born and raised in New Delhi, India, where he completed the International Baccalaureate programme at considerable personal cost. Classmates recall him as "a nuisance" — a characterisation multiple teachers independently arrived at without apparent coordination.[4] Despite this, or perhaps in defiance of it, Verma elected to study French, a decision he has since been unable to satisfactorily explain to any interviewer, other than "kurwa."[5]

During his secondary education, Verma developed an early enthusiasm for digital networking, maintaining an unusually active presence on WhatsApp that his family described as "concerning" and that he himself described as "socialising."[6] It was through this platform that he initiated contact with a Polish researcher whom he would later describe, in court documents and birthday cards alike, as his future wife.[7]

Following the IB, Verma secured a place at Nanyang Technological University in Singapore. His subsequent arrival in Singapore for his masters was marked by a brief period of housing fluidity — approximately one week — during which he lodged with a Polish "baddie" he had, at that point, met exclusively via WhatsApp. Scholars of attachment theory have cited this as "bold."[8]

Academic career

Quantum gambling theory

Verma's primary research interest lies at the intersection of quantum information theory and probabilistic risk — a field he has informally branded "quantum gambling." His thesis, provisionally titled Why I Always Lose in Buckshot Roulette with My Girlfriend, proposes that quantum superposition principles can be applied to predict outcomes in games of chance, provided the researcher is, in Verma's words, "built different."[9]

The paper has yet to be peer reviewed. Verma attributes this to "the academic establishment not being ready." His supervisor has not commented publicly, and has privately expressed regrets about ever introducing him to Santa Claus.[10]

NTU Film career

In a development that surprised his department, Verma became a minor film personality at NTU despite possessing no prior acting experience and being, notably, not Chinese — a demographic that comprises the majority of NTU's film productions.[11] His film script underwent revision no fewer than five times, angering the film teacher who thought that the man should get it together.[12] Verma declined to comment on the nature of the film, stating that "the world is not ready."[13]

Athletic achievements

Verma was named NTU's Most Promising Swimmer for 2026, an award that has prompted calls within the university's athletics department to establish an Amateur Swimming Olympics specifically so that Verma might win something.[13] Critics have noted that "most promising" implies a future not yet arrived; Verma has stated he prefers to "keep them waiting."

His commitment to physical excellence has extended to other pursuits. In 2025, Verma attempted paragliding in an incident that sources close to the subject describe as "nearly catastrophic from a bladder perspective." Verma himself has denied all accounts of this event and has instead focused public attention on the fact that he likely blew his shot with a French girl in the usual manner of his documented blindness to female advances.[14]

Controversies

The Tan Incident (April 2026). Verma has been on three separate occasions invited to join the African Student Union. The auntie at the canteen failed to recognise him.[15]

The Signal Reading Controversy. Multiple independent sources have alleged that Verma demonstrates a clinically remarkable inability to interpret social cues from women, including — but not limited to — instances where the relevant party remained in his dormitory room until hours that his university's code of conduct would classify as "the following morning."[16]

The Dhurandhar Affair. In early 2026, leaked Discord messages revealed that Verma had maintained a private Pinterest board titled "Hair Goals" consisting entirely of photographs of Hamza. This discovery coincided with dermatologist reports suggesting early-stage androgenic alopecia in the subject, i.e. losing hair like crazy. His professor can attest to his remarkable powers of hair loss while he watched Verma struggle with a final.[17]

Discord Kitten Allegations. In 2022/23, Verma was alleged to have maintained a "Discord kitten" — an online companion who, in keeping with Discord kitten tradition, misrepresented her age. Verma has described the situation as "complicated" and has requested no further questions, adding only: "but she told me she was 12," a statement his legal representative (himself) has since advised him to retract.[18]

Personal life

Verma currently resides in Singapore, where he is engaged in quantum research and, by his own account, an ongoing state of yearning. His long-distance relationship with a Polish baddie — originating, as noted, from a WhatsApp conversation — has been described by the subject as "like a boomerang: it keeps coming back, harder each time. Just like me whenever I see her."[19]

In 2026, Verma obtained a United States visa. When asked why, he stated he wished to visit "the hood" where his partner was currently residing. Immigration historians have noted this as an unusual motivation for an F-1 adjacent visa application.[20]

Verma has expressed ambitions to become President of India, with a stated foreign policy goal of "sorting out Pakistan." He has not elaborated on the methodology. Political scientists at NTU have described his candidacy as "premature" and "in need of touching grass."[21]

He is known to keep irregular hours, has an adversarial relationship with alarm clocks, and is described by those close to him as "sometimes might be shit, can is can" — a philosophical position he reportedly endorses and wishes more people understood.[22]

Notable quotes

  • "Sometimes might be shit, can is can." — Verma, on literally anything[23]
  • "When I grow up I will be president of India and destroy Pakistan." — Verma, at an NTU international students mixer, 2024[24]
  • "She was just staying late to use my charger." — Verma, 2023, on the Signal Reading Controversy[25]
  • "The hair board was for a class project." — Verma, 2026[26]
  • "Damn, baby." — Verma, default phrase; nearly deployed in the direction of his research supervisor, prompting outrage from his wife.[27]
